# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Build and Test Community Contributions

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize]

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          repository: ${{ github.repository }}

      - name: Setup Node.js environment
        uses: actions/setup-node@v1
        with:
          node-version: '14'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test

      - name: Post results as comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v5
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const { Octokit } = require('@octokit/core');
            const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN });
            const pullRequestNumber = context.pullRequest.number;
            const commentBody = `Automated test results for PR #${pullRequestNumber}: \n\n ${context.result}`;
            octokit.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.name,
              issue_number: pullRequestNumber,
              body: commentBody
            });
